questions.text: In a capillary tube, water rises to a height of 4 cm. If the cross-sectional area of the tube were one-fourth, water would have risen to a height of :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.text: A capillary tube (P) is dipped in water. Another identical tube (Q) is dipped in a soap-water solution. Which of the following shows the relative nature of the liquid columns in the two tubes?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The weight of the water risen in capillary tube is balanced by the force exerted due to decrease in pressure inside the water column due to the curvature at the water surface. The decrease in pressure=2σ/ r​ Hence from balancing the pressure, we get 2σ/r​=hρg Hence h∝σ Since surface tension(σ) of water decreases as soap is mixed, the height upto which water rises decreases.

The capillary rise is given by,h=2Tcosθ/ρrg ...(i).The soap solution and water both wet the side of the capillary tube.⇒The angle of contact for soap solution with glass, and the angle of contact for water with glass will both be acute (θ<90∘).Hence, the shape of the meniscus for water and soap both will be concave (concavity being higher for water-meniscus)Since soap solution has a greater tendency to stick to the wall of the tube, the angle of contact for soap-solution will be lesser.i.e θsoap<θwaterThe surface tension of soap solution is reduced to a much higher extent (i.e approx 3 times lesser then that of water), due to presence of surfactants.i.e Tsoap<TwaterThe increase in value of cosθ for soap solution will be lesser compared to the reduction in its surface tension, because both solutions are wetting in nature.Hence from Eq.(i) due to dominance of decrease in surface tension, hsoap<hwaterThe value of height h to which the liquid rises in the capillary tube will be less for soap solution than for water.∴ Higher rise of liquid in A with concave meniscus, represents water.

In case of water, the meniscus shape is concave upwards. Also according to the ascent formula h=2Tcosθ/rρg ​. The surface tension (T) of soap solution is less than water. Therefore rise of soap solution in the capillary tube is less as compared to water. As in the case of water, the meniscus shape of the soap solution is also concave upwards.

In the case of water, the meniscus shape is concave upwards. Also according to the ascent formula h=2Tcosθ/rρg. ​The surface tension (T) of soap solution is less than water. Therefore rise of soap solution in the capillary tube is less as compared to water. As in the case of water, the meniscus shape of the soap solution is also concave upwards.

questions.explanation: The capillary rise is given by, h=2Tcosθ/ρrg ...(i). The soap solution and water both wet the side of the capillary tube. ⇒The angle of contact for soap solution with glass, and the angle of contact for water with glass will both be acute (θ<90∘). Hence, the shape of the meniscus for water and soap both will be concave (concavity being higher for water-meniscus) Since soap solution has a greater tendency to stick to the wall of the tube, the angle of contact for soap-solution will be lesser. i.e θsoap<θwater The surface tension of soap solution is reduced to a much higher extent (i.e approx 3 times lesser then that of water), due to presence of surfactants. i.e Tsoap<Twater The increase in value of cosθ for soap solution will be lesser compared to the reduction in its surface tension, because both solutions are wetting in nature. Hence from Eq.(i) due to dominance of decrease in surface tension, hsoap<hwater The value of height h to which the liquid rises in the capillary tube will be less for soap solution than for water. ∴ Higher rise of liquid in A with concave meniscus, represents water.
hints: relative nature of the liquid columns in the two tubes

questions.text: A conical pipe shown in the figure has a water drop. The drop will tend to move towards:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The correct answer is a)Tapered end. The water drop will tend to move towards the tapered end of the conical pipe. This happens because the pressure in a fluid decreases as the cross-sectional area increases (according to Bernoulli's principle). Therefore, the water drop will move from the wider end (lower pressure) towards the tapered end (higher pressure).
This is not correct. The water drop will not move toward the wider end. In fact, it will move away from the wider end due to the pressure difference.
This option is not accurate. The direction of motion is determined by the pressure difference and will typically be toward the tapered end.

This is not the case. There will be a tendency for the water drop to move, as explained above. In summary, the water drop will tend to move towards the tapered end of the conical pipe due to the pressure difference created by the varying cross-sectional area.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is a)Tapered end. The water drop will tend to move towards the tapered end of the conical pipe. This happens because the pressure in a fluid decreases as the cross-sectional area increases (according to Bernoulli's principle). Therefore, the water drop will move from the wider end (lower pressure) towards the tapered end (higher pressure).

questions.text: Two solid spherical balls of radius r1 & r2 (r2 < r1) and density σ are tied up with a string and released in a viscous liquid of lesser density ρ and coefficient of viscosity η, with the string just taut as shown. The terminal velocity of the spheres is:

questions.text: Viscosity is closely related to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Viscosity and friction share similarities. Viscosity is a measure of a fluid's resistance to flow, primarily due to internal friction between its molecules. On the other hand, friction typically refers to the resistance to relative motion between two solid surfaces in contact.

Viscosity is primarily influenced by the adhesive forces between molecules of a fluid and the surface it is in contact with. These adhesive forces contribute to the resistance of the fluid to flow and are a key factor in viscosity.
Cohesive forces are the attractive forces between molecules of the same substance. While cohesive forces play a role in a fluid's behavior, they are not the primary factor in determining viscosity.

Bernoulli's theorem is a principle in fluid dynamics that relates the pressure, velocity, and height of a fluid in a streamline. While Bernoulli's theorem is relevant to fluid flow, it doesn't directly address viscosity, which is more related to the internal friction and molecular interactions within a fluid.

questions.explanation: Viscosity and friction share similarities. Viscosity is a measure of a fluid's resistance to flow, primarily due to internal friction between its molecules. On the other hand, friction typically refers to the resistance to relative motion between two solid surfaces in contact.

questions.text: With the increase in temperature, the viscosity of a liquid:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is not correct. In most cases, as the temperature of a liquid increases, its viscosity tends to decrease. This phenomenon is particularly evident in liquids like water, where higher temperatures lead to reduced viscosity.

This is the correct answer. An increase in temperature causes the molecules in a liquid to gain more kinetic energy and move more rapidly. This increased molecular motion disrupts the attractive forces between molecules, reducing the resistance to flow, and thereby decreasing viscosity.
While there are exceptions in certain situations and for specific fluids, the general trend is for the viscosity of a liquid to decrease as temperature rises.
This is not correct. There is indeed a relationship between temperature and the viscosity of a liquid, as explained above. Temperature changes can significantly affect the viscosity of most liquids.

questions.explanation: This is the correct answer. An increase in temperature causes the molecules in a liquid to gain more kinetic energy and move more rapidly. This increased molecular motion disrupts the attractive forces between molecules, reducing the resistance to flow, and thereby decreasing viscosity.

questions.text: A uniform cube is subjected to volume compression. If each side is decreased by 1.5%, then the bulk strain is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the following solution: If the side cube is l, then volume V=l3 For a change in volume ΔV/V = 3ld/l ∴ % Change in volume =3× % change in length =3×1% Hence, bulk strain =ΔV/V =0.03

questions.text: A body floats in a liquid contained in a beaker. The whole system, as shown, falls freely under gravity. The upthrust on the body due to the liquid is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer according to Archimedes' principle. The upthrust on a body submerged in a liquid is equal to the weight of the liquid displaced by the body. This is a fundamental principle in fluid mechanics.

options.text: Equal to the weight of the liquid displaced
options.explanation: This is not correct. When a body is submerged in a liquid, there is an upward force acting on it due to the buoyant force or upthrust.

options.text: Equal to the weight of the body in air
options.explanation: This is not correct. The upthrust is not equal to the weight of the body in air but rather equal to the weight of the liquid displaced.

options.text: Equal to the weight of the immersed position of the body
options.explanation: This is not correct. The upthrust is equal to the weight of the liquid displaced, not the weight of the immersed position of the body.

questions.explanation: This is the correct answer according to Archimedes' principle. The upthrust on a body submerged in a liquid is equal to the weight of the liquid displaced by the body. This is a fundamental principle in fluid mechanics.

questions.text: What is the Young's modulus of elasticity for a perfectly rigid body?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Young's modulus measures the stiffness or rigidity of a material, but it doesn't apply to perfectly rigid bodies, and its value does not extend to infinity. Since strain is zero therefore Y is infinite.

Young's modulus represents the ability of a material to deform elastically under an applied force. A perfectly rigid body does not deform at all, so the concept of Young's modulus does not apply, and its value is not zero.

Assigning a value of 1 to Young's modulus for a perfectly rigid body doesn't make physical sense. Young's modulus is typically measured in Pascals (Pa) and is associated with the material's properties.

Young's modulus is a positive value and cannot be negative, and it doesn't apply to perfectly rigid bodies. In essence, Young's modulus is a material property that describes how much a material deforms under the influence of an applied force. For a perfectly rigid body, no deformation occurs, and Young's modulus is not applicable. It's more relevant for materials that exhibit elastic behavior when subjected to stress.

questions.explanation: Young's modulus measures the stiffness or rigidity of a material, but it doesn't apply to perfectly rigid bodies, and its value does not extend to infinity. Since strain is zero therefore Y is infinite.

questions.text: Two stretched membranes of area 2 cm2 and 3 cm2 are placed in a liquid at the same depth. The ratio of pressures on them is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. According to Pascal's law, when two stretched membranes are placed at the same depth in a liquid, the pressure on them is determined by the depth and the density of the liquid, but it's not influenced by the areas of the membranes. Therefore, the ratio of the pressures on the two membranes is 1:1, meaning they experience the same pressure.

This option is not correct. The areas of the membranes do not affect the pressure at a given depth in a liquid. Therefore, the ratio of the pressures on the two membranes is not determined by their areas, and it's not 2:3.
This option is not correct for the same reason as option b. The ratio of the pressures on the two membranes is not determined by their areas, and it's not 3:2.

This option is not correct. It appears to be a mathematical expression of the ratio, but it doesn't represent the actual physical relationship between the pressures on the membranes. As mentioned earlier, the areas of the membranes do not affect the pressure, and therefore, the ratio of the pressures is 1:1.

questions.explanation: This is the correct answer. According to Pascal's law, when two stretched membranes are placed at the same depth in a liquid, the pressure on them is determined by the depth and the density of the liquid, but it's not influenced by the areas of the membranes. Therefore, the ratio of the pressures on the two membranes is 1:1, meaning they experience the same pressure. Pressure is independent of area of cross section.
hints: placed in a liquid at the same depth

questions.text: Water enters through end A with speed v1 and leaves through end B with speed v2 of a cylindrical tube AB. The tube is always completely filled with water. In case I the tube is horizontal and in case II it is vertical with end A upwards and in case III it is vertical with end B upwards. We have v1=v2 for:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In a horizontal tube (case I), with the tube oriented such that it is completely filled with water and there is no significant change in height, the velocities v1 and v2 can be equal. This is because the pressure at both ends A and B is approximately the same, as the height difference between them is minimal. Therefore, v1 = v2 could be true for case I.

In a horizontal tube (case II), with the tube oriented such that it is completely filled with water and there is no significant change in height, the velocities v1 and v2 can be equal. This is because the pressure at both ends A and B is approximately the same, as the height difference between them is minimal. Therefore, v1 = v2 could be true for case II.

In a horizontal tube (case III), with the tube oriented such that it is completely filled with water and there is no significant change in height, the velocities v1 and v2 can be equal. This is because the pressure at both ends A and B is approximately the same, as the height difference between them is minimal. Therefore, v1 = v2 could be true for case III.

The correct option is D. each case. Take any orientation of cylindrical tube [uniform cross section] Now, in any orientation the continuity equation is valid. Therefore, A1v1=A2v2 But A1=A2 ⇒v1=v2 Hence option (D) is correct. This happens in accordance with the equation of continuity and this equation was derived on the principle of conservation of mass and it is true in every case, whether the tube remains horizontal or vertical.

questions.explanation: The correct option is D. each case. Take any orientation of cylindrical tube [uniform cross section] Now, in any orientation the continuity equation is valid. Therefore, A1v1=A2v2 But A1=A2 ⇒v1=v2 Hence option (D) is correct. This happens in accordance with the equation of continuity and this equation was derived on the principle of conservation of mass and it is true in every case, whether the tube remains horizontal or vertical.
hints: Water enters through end A with speed v
leaves through end B with speed v
tube is always completely filled with water
case I the tube is horizontal
case II it is vertical with end A upwards
case III it is vertical with end B upwards
